_source: Scikit-learn documentation, Kaggle competition strategies, MLOps best practices
_note: Long-tail data science knowledge. Feature engineering, model selection, validation strategies, production considerations.

scaling_methods.standardization: z = (x - μ) / σ; centers at 0, std 1; for algorithms assuming normal distribution (linear, logistic regression)
scaling_methods.min_max_normalization: x' = (x - min) / (max - min); scales to [0,1]; preserves zero; for neural networks, image data
scaling_methods.robust_scaling: Uses median and IQR instead of mean/std; resistant to outliers; for data with extreme values
scaling_methods.log_transform: log(x+1); for right-skewed data; income, population, counts; makes multiplicative relationships additive

binning_discretization.equal_width: Split range into equal intervals; simple but sensitive to outliers
binning_discretization.equal_frequency: Quantile-based; each bin has same number of samples; better for skewed distributions
binning_discretization.domain_knowledge: Age groups: 0-18, 18-30, 30-50, 50+; income brackets; custom business logic

polynomial_features.interaction_terms: x1 * x2; captures non-linear relationships; quadratic (x²) for curves
polynomial_features.example: predicting house price: size * location_score interaction; size² for diminishing returns on very large homes
polynomial_features.caution: Degree 3+ creates feature explosion; 10 features → 1000+ polynomial features; use sparse models or regularization

encoding_methods.one_hot_encoding: Binary column per category; for nominal data (color, country); use when <10-15 categories
encoding_methods.label_encoding: Integer 0,1,2,...; only for ordinal data (small/medium/large); tree models can use for nominal if cardinality high
encoding_methods.target_encoding: Replace category with mean target value; powerful but overfits; must use CV to encode train set
encoding_methods.frequency_encoding: Replace with count or percentage; treats common categories differently; for high-cardinality features
encoding_methods.embedding: Neural network learned representation; for high-cardinality (user_id, product_id); converts to dense vector

handling_rare_categories.threshold_grouping: Categories appearing <1% of time → 'Other' bin; reduces dimensionality
handling_rare_categories.hierarchical_encoding: Use category hierarchy; city→state→country; encode multiple levels

time_series_features.lag_features: Previous values: t-1, t-7, t-30; sales_yesterday, sales_last_week
time_series_features.rolling_statistics: 7-day moving average; 30-day rolling std; captures trends and volatility
time_series_features.date_features: Day of week (0-6), month (1-12), quarter, is_weekend, is_holiday, days_since_event
time_series_features.cyclical_encoding: sin/cos transform for month, day of week; preserves circular nature (Dec and Jan are close)

text_features.bag_of_words: CountVectorizer; word counts; sparse matrix; simple baseline
text_features.tfidf: TF-IDF weights; downweights common words; better than raw counts for most tasks
text_features.n_grams: Bigrams (2-word), trigrams; captures 'not good' vs 'good'; increases dimensionality
text_features.embeddings: Word2Vec, GloVe, BERT; pre-trained semantic vectors; transfer learning

linear_regression.use_when: Linear relationship; interpretability required; fast prediction
linear_regression.assumptions: Linearity, independence, homoscedasticity, normality of residuals
linear_regression.regularization: Ridge (L2): shrinks coefficients; Lasso (L1): feature selection (drives coefficients to 0); ElasticNet: combination

tree_based.random_forest: Ensemble of decision trees; handles non-linearity; robust to outliers; no scaling needed; interpretable via feature importance
tree_based.gradient_boosting: XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost; sequential trees correcting errors; best tabular data performance; hyperparameter-sensitive
tree_based.xgboost_tips: max_depth 3-10; learning_rate 0.01-0.3; n_estimators 100-1000; colsample_bytree 0.3-0.8 prevents overfitting

neural_networks.mlp: Multi-layer perceptron; 2-3 hidden layers; ReLU activation; for complex non-linear patterns
neural_networks.when_to_use: Large datasets (>10k samples); complex interactions; time to tune; GPU available

classification.logistic_regression: Binary/multi-class; outputs probabilities; interpretable coefficients; baseline model
classification.svm: Support Vector Machine; kernel trick for non-linearity; good for high-dimensional data; slow on large datasets
classification.naive_bayes: Fast; works well with text; assumes feature independence (often violated but still works)
classification.lightgbm: Faster than XGBoost; handles categorical features natively; best for large datasets (>100k rows)
classification.catboost: Handles categorical features without encoding; prevents overfitting on categoricals; slower training but good accuracy

techniques.class_weights: sklearn class_weight='balanced'; penalizes misclassifying minority class more
techniques.smote: Synthetic Minority Oversampling; creates synthetic minority samples; use with caution (can create unrealistic samples)
techniques.undersampling: Random undersample majority class; fast but loses data; use when majority class is huge
techniques.threshold_tuning: Default 0.5 threshold; shift to 0.3 to catch more positives; use precision-recall curve to optimize

metrics.accuracy_misleading: 99% accuracy on 1% fraud dataset = predicting all negative
metrics.precision_recall: Precision = TP/(TP+FP); Recall = TP/(TP+FN); F1 = harmonic mean; prioritize based on cost of FP vs FN
metrics.roc_auc: Area under ROC curve; threshold-independent; good for comparing models
metrics.pr_auc: Precision-Recall AUC; better than ROC for imbalanced data

train_test_split.standard: 80/20 or 70/30 split; stratify=y for classification; random_state for reproducibility
train_test_split.time_series: No random split! Use time-based split; train on past, test on future; prevents data leakage

cross_validation.k_fold: 5 or 10 folds; shuffled; gives mean and std of performance; more reliable than single split
cross_validation.stratified_k_fold: Preserves class distribution in each fold; essential for imbalanced data
cross_validation.group_k_fold: Groups (e.g., user_id) stay together; prevents same user in train and test; avoids leakage
cross_validation.time_series_cv: TimeSeriesSplit; expanding window; respects temporal order; essential for forecasting

data_leakage_prevention.fit_on_train_only: fit_transform(X_train); transform(X_test); NEVER fit scaler on full dataset
data_leakage_prevention.target_leakage: Feature contains information unavailable at prediction time; purchase_amount predicting purchase_binary
data_leakage_prevention.group_leakage: Same entity in train and test; user_id, patient_id; use GroupKFold or time-based split
data_leakage_prevention.preprocessing_in_pipeline: Use sklearn Pipeline; ensures correct fit/transform order; prevents accidental leakage

grid_search.method: GridSearchCV; exhaustive search over parameter grid
grid_search.pros: Complete coverage; finds global optimum in grid
grid_search.use_when: Few parameters; known good ranges; small dataset

random_search.pros: Faster; explores wider space; better than grid if interactions unknown
random_search.distributions: Use loguniform for learning_rate (0.001-0.3); uniform for subsample (0.5-1.0)

bayesian_optimization.method: Optuna, Hyperopt; builds probabilistic model of objective function
bayesian_optimization.pros: Fewer iterations than random; focuses on promising regions; handles conditional parameters
bayesian_optimization.use_when: Expensive model training (neural networks); >5 hyperparameters; need best performance

model_serialization.pickle: import pickle; pickle.dump(model, file); standard but version-sensitive
model_serialization.joblib: from joblib import dump, load; faster than pickle for large numpy arrays; sklearn recommended
model_serialization.onnx: Cross-platform; deploy sklearn/pytorch model to C++/Java; standardized format

data_drift.definition: Input distribution changes over time; feature mean/std shifts; new categories appear
data_drift.detection: KL divergence, KS test between train and production data; alert if p<0.05
data_drift.mitigation: Retrain monthly/quarterly; add new data; update feature engineering

concept_drift.definition: Relationship between X and y changes; fraud patterns evolve; customer preferences shift
concept_drift.detection: Monitor model performance on ground truth; precision/recall declining over time
concept_drift.mitigation: Online learning; sliding window retraining; A/B test new model

feature_store.purpose: Centralized feature computation and storage; ensures train/serve consistency
feature_store.tools: Feast, Tecton, AWS SageMaker Feature Store
feature_store.benefits: Reuse features across models; versioning; point-in-time correctness for time series

ab_testing.champion_challenger: Route 95% traffic to current model, 5% to new model; compare metrics
ab_testing.sample_size: Calculate required N for statistical power; typically 1000+ predictions per variant
ab_testing.duration: Run 1-2 weeks minimum; account for weekly seasonality
ab_testing.success_criteria: Pre-define threshold (e.g., +5% F1 score, p<0.05); avoid peeking

common_mistakes.data_leakage: Most common failure; fit scaler on full data; target info in features; same entity in train/test
common_mistakes.not_validating_properly: Single train/test split; no CV; optimistic results; fails in production
common_mistakes.ignoring_class_imbalance: Using accuracy on 1:99 dataset; predicting majority class gets 99% accuracy
common_mistakes.feature_engineering_after_split: Computing stats on full dataset then splitting; leaks information
common_mistakes.not_handling_missing_values: Model crashes on null; or fills with mean computed on full dataset (leakage)
common_mistakes.overfitting_to_test_set: Tuning hyperparameters on test set; use train/val/test or nested CV
common_mistakes.ignoring_business_context: Optimizing F1 when cost of FP ≠ cost of FN; align metric with business objective
